It's interesting to see how Fred Gaisberg, as a pianist, was able to understand the perspective of the artist and made himself familiar with the recording technology. I also find it intriguing that the tasks of a toningeur, music producer, and artist-and-repertoire manager were often combined in one person at that time. This is an interesting contrast to the way private companies operate. I'm also intrigued by the idea of Doppik, which seems to be a more modern approach to public accounting.
